response:
This error occurs because your WPF project is implicitly targeting the older .NET Framework runtime while your main application runs on .NET 7. You must explicitly configure your WPF project to target .NET 7 or a compatible cross-platform version to ensure the correct assemblies are loaded on Windows.

cousin projects often share similar dependency issues when mixing different runtime targets so you need to verify that your WPF project file specifies the correct target framework version in the PropertyGroup section.

Ensure that you have the Microsoft.NET.Sdk.WindowsDesktop package installed in your WPF project file to enable WPF support within the modern .NET SDK environment. This package provides the necessary bindings and references for WPF controls to function correctly on .NET 7 without falling back to legacy .NET Framework paths.

You should also check your project references to confirm that you are not accidentally referencing old .NET Framework assemblies directly which can cause version mismatches and loading failures. Updating all NuGet packages to their latest versions compatible with .NET 7 will often resolve these missing file errors automatically.

Finally rebuild your entire solution after making these changes to ensure that all binaries are generated against the correct runtime version and that the Windows platform can locate the PresentationFramework assembly successfully.
